evidence: The set B is defined by forbidding residues: m not congruent to x (mod n) for all x in X_n, a subset of Z/nZ chosen for each n.
The question asks whether B must have a logarithmic density (HasLogDensity), the log-weighted variant of natural/asymptotic density.
Docstring: when A = {p^2 : p prime}, B is the set of squarefree numbers, and Erdős proved the gap-sum limit exists in this case.
The multiset A_k of all sums of k distinct elements of A (sumMultiset via powersetCard) is the restricted-sumset data determining A in the Selfridge-Straus problem.
Docstring states 'This is also known as the Littlewood conjecture' and the decl states liminf n ||n alpha|| ||n beta|| = 0 for all real alpha, beta.
The statement bounds n * distToNearestInt(n*alpha) * distToNearestInt(n*beta), the central quantity of simultaneous Diophantine approximation; erdosproblems.com tags it 'diophantine approximation'.
The statement quantifies over all real n x n doubly stochastic matrices (M in doublyStochastic R (Fin n)) and asks for a permutation diagonal with product at least n^-n.
The van der Waerden conjecture decl bounds M.permanent below by n^-n * n! for doubly stochastic M.
Docstring: 'the permanent of a doubly stochastic matrix is at least n^-n n!', proved by Gyires, Egorychev, and Falikman.
The problem concerns the asymptotic distribution function of phi(n)/n (IsDistributionOfPhiRatio uses Nat.totient) and whether its derivative is ever positive.
IsDistributionOfPhiRatio defines f(c) as the natural density of {n : phi(n) < cn} via Set.HasDensity.
Docstring: Erdős [Er95] proved the distribution function of phi(n)/n is purely singular - continuous with derivative zero almost everywhere (IsPurelySingular).
The hypothesis is that each bounded set A_x has Lebesgue outer measure < 1 (volume.toOuterMeasure (A x) < 1).
Docstring: Hechler [He72] showed the answer is no assuming the continuum hypothesis; the decl hypothesizes aleph_1 = continuum.
